##NAMES=Gaspeite

##LOCALITY=Lemieux Township, Gaspe Nord County, Gaspe Peninsula, Quebec, Canada
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=University of Arizona Mineral Museum
##DESCRIPTION=Green massive
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R040157
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Ni_0.54_Mg_0.31_Fe_0.15_Ca_0.01_Co_0.01_)C_1.00_O_3_ ; Gray and light grey bands are gaspéite. The lightest bands are: (Ni_0.57_Mg_0.41_Fe_0.08_)C_0.97_O_3_  The dark grey bands are magnesite: (Mg_0.98_Fe_0.04_Ni_0.03_)C_0.97_O_3_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 4.6223 b: 4.6223 c: 14.915 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 120 volume: 275.9 crystal system: hexagonal
